content:
# Daily Closing Checklist

**Tool ID:** T093-01
**Block:** B093 (Closing Checklist)
**Domain:** Operations & Workflow (D05_operations_workflow)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** Shift Lead
**Time Required:** 10-15 minutes (verification); 45-60 minutes (full closing procedure)
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. A good close sets up a good open. Every shortcut tonight becomes a problem at 06:30 tomorrow morning.

Not another tick-box exercise. This checklist exists because the details it covers are the ones that slip when you are busy -- and the ones that matter most when they do.

**Built for Shift Lead working in Operations & Workflow.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Laminated A4 checklist covering all closing tasks grouped by zone: Front of House, Bar, Kitchen/Prep, Cash and Admin, and Security. Includes tick boxes, sign-off line, date, and time of completion.

This tool supports **Closing Checklist** (B093) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Shift Lead (verifying), Closing Team (executing)
**Time Required:** Full closing procedure: 45-60 minutes (team of 2). Checklist verification: 10-15 minutes.
**Frequency:** Every single day at close.

**What You Will Need:**
- This checklist (laminated at the closing station)
- Cleaning supplies (sanitiser, cloths, mop, bin bags)
- Cash counting sheet
- T094-01 Handover Log (to complete for the morning team)
- Keys and alarm code

**Steps:**
1. Begin closing tasks 30 minutes before scheduled close (soft close)
2. Last customer served by closing time -- do not rush anyone out
3. After last customer leaves, work through the checklist zone by zone
4. Each team member can take a zone -- Shift Lead verifies all
5. Complete the T094-01 Handover Log with any issues for the morning team
6. Final walkthrough by Shift Lead before setting alarm and locking up

---

## Checklist

### Closer Information

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Date | |
| Day of week | |
| Shift Lead (closer) | |
| Closing team members | |
| Last customer served at | |
| Target lockup time | |

### Zone 1: FRONT OF HOUSE

| # | Task | Done | Notes |
|---|------|------|-------|
| 1.1 | All tables cleared, wiped, and sanitised | | |
| 1.2 | Chairs stacked or pushed in neatly | | |
| 1.3 | Condiments, sugar, napkin holders restocked for morning | | |
| 1.4 | Floor swept and mopped (under tables and along walls) | | |
| 1.5 | Windows and door glass wiped (inside) | | |
| 1.6 | Customer toilets cleaned, restocked (paper, soap), floor mopped | | |
| 1.7 | A-board / outdoor furniture brought in and stored | | |
| 1.8 | All customer-facing surfaces wiped (windowsills, shelves, counter front) | | |

### Zone 2: BAR (Espresso and Drinks Station)

| # | Task | Done | Notes |
|---|------|------|-------|
| 2.1 | Espresso machine backflushed (chemical + water rinse -- see T048-01) | | |
| 2.2 | Steam wands deep-cleaned (purge, soak, wipe) | | |
| 2.3 | Grinder exterior wiped, hopper sealed or covered | | |
| 2.4 | Drip tray removed, cleaned, replaced | | |
| 2.5 | Knock box emptied, rinsed, replaced | | |
| 2.6 | Bar counter wiped and sanitised | | |
| 2.7 | Milk jugs washed and stored upside down to dry | | |
| 2.8 | Opened milk returned to fridge (check use-by -- discard if expired) | | |
| 2.9 | Syrup pumps wiped, bottles capped | | |
| 2.10 | Bar floor swept and mopped | | |
| 2.11 | Dishwasher / glasswasher: run final cycle, empty, wipe down, leave door ajar | | |
| 2.12 | All cups, saucers, glassware washed and put away | | |

### Zone 3: KITCHEN / PREP AREA

| # | Task | Done | Notes |
|---|------|------|-------|
| 3.1 | All food stored correctly: covered, labelled, dated, in fridge | | |
| 3.2 | Prep surfaces wiped and sanitised | | |
| 3.3 | Chopping boards cleaned, sanitised, stored vertically | | |
| 3.4 | Display case emptied, food stored or discarded (date-check first) | | |
| 3.5 | Display case wiped inside and turned off (or left running per policy) | | |
| 3.6 | Oven / toaster turned off and wiped | | |
| 3.7 | Food waste bin emptied and bag replaced | | |
| 3.8 | Kitchen floor swept and mopped | | |
| 3.9 | Handwash sink: restocked soap and paper towels for morning | | |

### Zone 4: CASH AND ADMIN

| # | Task | Done | Notes |
|---|------|------|-------|
| 4.1 | POS end-of-day report run and printed/saved | | |
| 4.2 | Cash counted and reconciled against POS report | | |
| 4.3 | Cash discrepancy noted (if any): +/- EUR _____ | | |
| 4.4 | Cash secured (safe or bank deposit bag) | | |
| 4.5 | Card payment terminal daily settlement run | | |
| 4.6 | Float set for tomorrow: EUR _____ | | |
| 4.7 | Any refunds or voids noted and explained | | |
| 4.8 | Delivery notes filed / supplier invoices stored | | |

### Zone 5: SECURITY AND FINAL WALKTHROUGH

| # | Task | Done | Notes |
|---|------|------|-------|
| 5.1 | All waste bags taken to external bins | | |
| 5.2 | Recycling sorted and stored correctly | | |
| 5.3 | Back door locked and secured | | |
| 5.4 | All windows closed and locked | | |
| 5.5 | Fridge and freezer doors fully closed (check seal) | | |
| 5.6 | All non-essential electrical equipment turned off | | |
| 5.7 | Espresso machine left ON / turned OFF (per house policy: _____) | | |
| 5.8 | Lights off (except security lights if applicable) | | |
| 5.9 | T094-01 Handover Log completed for morning team | | |
| 5.10 | Final walkthrough: nothing looks wrong, smells wrong, or sounds wrong | | |
| 5.11 | Alarm set | | |
| 5.12 | Front door locked, keys secured | | |

### Summary

| Zone | Items | Done | Issues |
|------|-------|------|--------|
| 1. Front of House | 8 | | |
| 2. Bar | 12 | | |
| 3. Kitchen / Prep | 9 | | |
| 4. Cash and Admin | 8 | | |
| 5. Security | 12 | | |
| **TOTAL** | **49** | | |

**Sign-Off:**
- Date: _______________
- Shift Lead: _______________
- Closing completed at: _______________
- Premises locked at: _______________
- Signature: _______________
- Handover log completed? Yes / No

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Rushing Closing Because Staff Want to Leave

**Why it happens:** It has been a long day. Closing team wants to finish fast. Sanitising becomes a quick wipe. Backflush is skipped. Floor is swept but not mopped.
**How to prevent:** Closing time is paid time. Staff should not be rushing to leave unpaid. Set expectations: "We close properly. It takes 45-60 minutes. That is the job."
**If it is already happening:** Do a surprise morning check. Arrive at 06:30 and assess how the closing team left the cafe. If the bar is sticky or the floor is dirty, address it directly with the closing Shift Lead.

### Risk 2: Cash Count Discrepancies Not Investigated

**Why it happens:** Till is EUR 2 short. Closer notes it and moves on. Over a month, small discrepancies add up. Nobody investigates.
**How to prevent:** Any discrepancy over EUR 2 must be explained. Check for voided transactions, refunds, or miscounted float. Record every discrepancy in item 4.3.
**If it is already happening:** Review the last 2 weeks of cash counts. If discrepancies trend in one direction, investigate patterns (same closing team? same day of week?).

### Risk 3: Handover Log Not Completed

**Why it happens:** Closing team finishes the physical tasks but does not write the handover. Morning team arrives to discover the dishwasher is broken, a delivery did not arrive, or a customer complaint was not logged.
**How to prevent:** Item 5.9 is mandatory. The T094-01 Handover Log is the LAST task before the alarm. No log = closing not complete.
**If it is already happening:** Make it the Shift Lead's personal responsibility. The handover log is signed by the closer -- it is their professional standard.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add filter equipment closing (empty batch brewer, clean pour-over station, store V60s).
**Chain / Franchise:** Align with brand closing standards. Add brand-specific items (promotional material rotation, digital screen shutdown).
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Replace Zones 1 and 3 with vehicle/stall breakdown. Add equipment storage and vehicle security checks.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add bakery closing: oven cool-down procedure, dough disposal or storage for proving, display case deep clean.

**Making it yours:**
- Laminate and mount on the wall behind the bar
- Time your closing procedure -- it should take 45-60 minutes with 2 people
- Rotate closing responsibilities so everyone can close competently
- Compare morning opening quality to closing quality -- a clean close makes a smooth open

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T093-02 -- Closing Procedures SOP (L2, SOP)
- T093-03 -- Morning Opening Quality Score Card (L2, tracker)
- T093-04 -- Closing Compliance Dashboard (L3, dashboard)

**Related blocks:**
- B092

**Parent block:** B093 -- Closing Checklist
**Module:** M05: Service Flow & Peak Management
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
